claim.summary: The delegated queue status command now exposes a native role_closeout block that classifies whether the role can close out without operator steering. Replaying r16 through scripts/run_job_queue.py status --json produced queue_empty_trusted with all 17 jobs completed successfully, trusted evidence, and zero active leases.

focused_tests.result: passed: full job queue suite, 73 passed in 25.73s, plus py_compile

code_changes: scripts/run_job_queue.py now derives queue.role_closeout from queue jobs, active leases, blockers, terminal outcomes, and the unattended trust ledger.
The plain status CLI now prints a role_closeout line with readiness, mode, job counts, lease count, trust status, and operator_steering_required.
tests/test_job_queue.py covers active-lease non-closeout, JSON role_closeout payload presence, and trusted queue-empty closeout.

isolated_cli_r17.command: scripts/run_job_queue.py status --json with r16 queue, runtime_state, reports, checkpoints, episodes, and workspace paths

open_limits: This packet closes the product-native status/closeout reporting gap over r16; it does not add new multi-hour runtime evidence.
The replay uses the r16 built-in task-bank workload and therefore does not remove the remaining full-A5 limits around less task-bank-bound intake.
Full production-duty A5 still needs longer role operation and product-native intake beyond deterministic known task selection.
